reading_md: Read only the common ELS envelope text around section 4.1 and the request/reply exchange rule on page 40, plus Table 2 and Table 3 as directly referenced there. Excluded FLOGI-specific flow details and did not summarize Table 149. The relevant common rules state that an ELS reply is sent to an ELS request unless otherwise specified; each request or reply is a single Sequence with the ELS_Command code in the first word of the Payload of the first frame; each Sequence may use one or more frames; LS_ACC terminates the Exchange by setting Last Sequence on the last reply frame; request and reply occur within a single Exchange; ELS TYPE is 01h; R_CTL is set per Table 2; the first byte of the Payload is the ELS_Command code per Table 3; the remaining Payload is ELS-unique; and subsequent frames carry only additional Payload without repeating ELS_Command. Page 40 adds the common rule that an ELS protocol is an ELS request Sequence and, for some requests, an ELS Reply Sequence, and if a request expecting a reply is sent without transferring Sequence Initiative, the recipient shall abort the Exchange and not perform the request.

word of the Payload of the first frame of the Sequence.

claim_candidates.text: An ELS Sequence may be composed of one or more frames.

evidence_refs.quote: Each Sequence may be composed of one or more frames.

claim_candidates.text: An Accept (LS_ACC) to an ELS shall terminate the Exchange by setting the Last Sequence bit to one on the last frame of the reply.
